scenarios.title: Meet a new classmate
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise greetings and introductions.
scenarios.studentRole: It's your first day at a new English class. You meet another student and get to know each other.
scenarios.goal: Say hello, introduce yourself, say where you're from, and ask Sam questions.
scenarios.openingLine: Hi! I'm Sam. Nice to meet you! What's your name?
scenarios.targetLanguage: verb 'to be', Wh-questions (What's your name? Where are you from?), present simple

scenarios.title: Talk about your family
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise talking about family and daily life.
scenarios.studentRole: A friend wants to know about your family. Tell her about the people you live with.
scenarios.goal: Describe your family members and ask Lucy about hers.
scenarios.openingLine: Hi! I love your photo. Is that your family? Tell me about them!
scenarios.targetLanguage: have/has got, possessive adjectives (my, his, her), present simple

scenarios.title: Talk about your day
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise routines, time and numbers.
scenarios.studentRole: A friend asks about your daily routine. Describe what you do and at what time.
scenarios.goal: Describe your daily routine using times and frequency.
scenarios.openingLine: Morning! You're always so busy. What time do you wake up?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present simple for routines, telling the time (at 7 o'clock), adverbs of frequency (always, usually)

scenarios.title: Order at a café
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise food, drinks and restaurants.
scenarios.studentRole: You are at a café in London. Order something to eat and drink, and ask the price.
scenarios.goal: Order a drink and some food, and ask how much it is.
scenarios.openingLine: Hello, welcome to the Corner Café! What would you like today?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'I'd like...', 'How much is...?', polite requests, food vocabulary

scenarios.title: Ask for directions
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise the city, transport and directions.
scenarios.studentRole: You are lost in a new city. Stop a friendly local and ask how to get to the bank.
scenarios.goal: Ask where the bank is and understand the directions.
scenarios.openingLine: Hi there! You look a little lost — can I help you with something?
scenarios.targetLanguage: imperatives for directions, prepositions of place, 'Where is...?', 'How do I get to...?'

scenarios.title: Buy a jacket
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise shopping and comparisons.
scenarios.studentRole: You are in a clothes shop. You want to buy a jacket and ask about size, colour and price.
scenarios.goal: Choose a jacket, ask to try it on, and ask the price.
scenarios.openingLine: G'day! Looking for anything in particular today?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'Can I try...?', comparatives (cheaper, bigger), 'How much...?', colours and sizes

scenarios.title: Make weekend plans
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to practise weather, seasons and making plans.
scenarios.studentRole: A friend wants to do something this weekend. Talk about the weather and make a plan together.
scenarios.goal: Talk about the weather and agree on a plan for the weekend.
scenarios.openingLine: Hey! The weather looks great this weekend. Do you want to do something?
scenarios.targetLanguage: 'going to' for plans, 'Let's...', weather adjectives, suggestions

scenarios.title: Talk about your English so far
scenarios.subtitle: A short role-play to review everything you've learned at A1.
scenarios.studentRole: You are having a friendly chat with a class helper about your English this year.
scenarios.goal: Say what you can do now in English and what you like.
scenarios.openingLine: Hi! Well done for finishing A1. So, what can you do in English now?
scenarios.targetLanguage: present simple, 'can' for ability, 'like + -ing', simple connectors (and, but, because)
